word: G2350 - θορυβέω - thorybéō - thor-oo-beh'-o - Make ado

sections.paragraph:
- Original: θορυβέω

- Transliteration: Thorubeo

- Phonetic: thor-oo-beh'-o

- Definition:

1. to make a noise or uproar, be turbulent

2. to disturb, throw into confusion

a. to be troubled in mind

b. to wail tumultuously

- Origin: from G2351

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G2351; to be in tumult that is disturb clamor: - make ado (a noise) trouble self set on an uproar.
